agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs how employees accrue paid leave, request time off, and how unused leave is handled at year-end. This is a classic accrual-based leave system with manager approval workflow and safeguards against balance overdraft.","bounded_context":"Leave Management — owns accrual calculations, leave balances, leave request lifecycle, and year-end carry-over. Interfaces with Employee/Contract context (contract end dates, employment start) and Organizational context (manager relationships).","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued but unused leave days an employee holds. Must never go negative."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which leave days are earned. Employees accrue 1.5 days per month worked."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal request by an employee to consume accrued leave days for a specified date range. Follows a lifecycle: Submitted → Approved/Rejected → (if approved) Consumed."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A special category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (after the absence) provided a doctor's note is attached within 5 calendar days of the absence."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and company-recognized holidays. Used to calculate the 3-day advance notice requirement."},{"term":"Carry-Over","definition":"The process at calendar year-end where unused leave days are evaluated. A maximum of 10 days may carry over; any excess is forfeited."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The permanent loss of accrued leave days that exceed the 10-day carry-over cap at calendar year-end."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's current employment contract terminates. Leave requests must not extend beyond this date."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical certificate required to validate retroactive medical leave submissions."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per completed month of work.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The accrual rate (1.5 days/month) is a company policy that could be changed by HR leadership. The mechanism of accrual is structural, but the rate is policy.","testable_assertion":"Given an employee who has worked N complete months, their total accrued leave equals N × 1.5 days (minus any consumed leave)."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An employee's leave balance must never go below zero. Leave cannot be taken before it is accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Negative balance would mean consuming a resource that does not exist — a state integrity violation. The system would be tracking phantom entitlements. This is a hard data consistency constraint.","testable_assertion":"At no point in time shall any employee's leave balance be less than zero. Any operation that would cause a negative balance must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Non-medical leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the requested leave start date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-working-day advance notice is an operational policy. Management could adjust this window without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A non-medical leave request submitted fewer than 3 working days before its start date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave may be submitted retroactively (after the absence dates) but must be submitted within 5 calendar days of the absence and must include a valid doctor's note.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 5-day window and documentation requirement are HR policy choices, not physical constraints. However, the linkage between retroactive submission and medical justification is a business-critical control.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request submitted more than 5 calendar days after the last day of absence is rejected. A medical leave request without an attached doctor's note is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only the employee's designated manager may approve or reject a leave request.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Authorization integrity — allowing unauthorized actors to approve leave would be a security and compliance violation that corrupts the approval chain.","testable_assertion":"A leave request can only transition to Approved or Rejected by an actor with the Manager role over the requesting employee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave requests do not consume any leave balance. Balance is only deducted upon approval.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If rejected leave consumed balance, the system would be in an inconsistent state — days subtracted without actual time off granted. This is a state integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request is rejected, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from its value before the request was submitted."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request's date range must not extend beyond the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"An employee cannot be on company leave after their contract has ended — this is a physical/legal impossibility. Allowing it would create phantom obligations.","testable_assertion":"A leave request where any requested day falls on or after the employee's contract end date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave days exceeding 10 are forfeited. A maximum of 10 days carries over to the next year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day cap is a company policy that could be adjusted. The concept of carry-over with a cap is structural, but the threshold is policy.","testable_assertion":"On January 1st, any employee whose previous year-end balance exceeded 10 days has their balance set to 10. Employees with 10 or fewer days retain their full balance."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked 4 complete months and taken 0 leave days","when":"The system calculates their leave balance","then":"The balance equals 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient balance' and the balance remains 2 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days and today is Monday","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request starting on Wednesday (2 working days away)","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient advance notice — minimum 3 working days required'"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days and today is Monday","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request starting on Thursday (3 working days away)","then":"The request is accepted for manager review"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent on Monday due to illness and today is Saturday (5 calendar days later)","when":"They submit a medical leave request for Monday with a doctor's note attached","then":"The request is accepted for manager review despite being retroactive"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was absent on Monday due to illness and today is the following Sunday (6 calendar days later)","when":"They submit a medical leave request for Monday","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'medical leave must be submitted within 5 calendar days of absence'"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a medical leave request retroactively within 5 days","when":"The request does not include a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'doctor's note required for retroactive medical leave'"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A manager reviews a pending leave request","when":"The manager rejects the request","then":"The employee's leave balance remains unchanged and the request status becomes 'Rejected'"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A manager reviews a pending leave request for 3 days from an employee with 5 days balance","when":"The manager approves the request","then":"The employee's leave balance is reduced to 2 days and the request status becomes 'Approved'"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 13–16","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'leave request extends beyond contract end date'"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carry-over process executes","then":"4 days are forfeited and the employee's balance on January 1 is 10 days"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carry-over process executes","then":"All 8 days carry over and the employee's balance on January 1 is 8 days"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request for exactly the contract end date (last working day)","expected_behavior":"Should be allowed — the contract end date is the first day they are NOT employed. If the spec means 'end date' is the last working day, leave ON that date should be permitted. Clarify whether contract end date is inclusive or exclusive."},{"scenario":"Medical leave spanning multiple days — does the 5-day retroactive window start from the first or last day of absence?","expected_behavior":"The 5-day window should start from the last day of the absence period, since the employee may not know total absence duration until they recover."},{"scenario":"Employee submits leave that would leave balance at exactly 0","expected_behavior":"Must be allowed — the rule is 'cannot go negative', not 'must maintain a minimum positive balance'. Zero is valid."},{"scenario":"Partial month worked — employee starts mid-month","expected_behavior":"Accrual rule says 'per month worked.' Partial months need a policy decision: pro-rata accrual, round up, or require full month completion. Currently unspecified."},{"scenario":"Leave request submitted 3 working days ahead but a holiday falls in between","expected_behavior":"The 3-day count uses working days, so holidays are excluded. If Monday is a holiday, a request submitted Friday for Thursday (next week) has only Tue/Wed as intervening working days — fails the 3-day rule."},{"scenario":"Carry-over interacts with pending approved leave spanning year-end","expected_behavior":"If an employee has 14 days balance and 5 days of approved leave starting Dec 29 through Jan 2, the system must determine whether forfeiture applies before or after the leave consumption. Business should clarify sequencing."},{"scenario":"Manager approves leave, then employee's contract end date is moved earlier","expected_behavior":"Previously approved leave that now extends past the new contract end date must be revoked or flagged. The system needs a reconciliation mechanism."},{"scenario":"Employee submits retroactive medical leave for a day they had previously submitted (and been rejected for) a regular leave request","expected_behavior":"Should be allowed — medical leave is a different category and the retroactive submission with doctor's note satisfies the medical leave rules independently."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Leave accrual creates a financial liability on the company's books. The carry-over cap at 10 days limits year-over-year liability accumulation. Incorrect balance tracking could lead to over-granting leave (lost productivity) or under-granting (employee dissatisfaction, legal risk). Forfeiture rules directly reduce financial liability at year-end.","operational":"Automated accrual and balance enforcement removes manual tracking burden from HR. The 3-day advance notice rule gives managers planning time for resource allocation. Medical leave exemption ensures the system accommodates genuine emergencies without penalizing employees.","user":"Employees gain transparency into their leave balance and entitlements. Clear rejection reasons prevent confusion. Retroactive medical leave with doctor's note prevents penalizing genuinely ill employees. The carry-over cap incentivizes employees to use leave (promoting well-being) while capping organizational risk.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Balance calculation errors leading to compliance violations; (2) Contract end-date enforcement failure creating phantom obligations; (3) Year-end forfeiture miscalculation leading to employee disputes. The no-negative-balance invariant is the most critical — its violation would indicate systemic corruption.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Direct users — their leave entitlements, balance accuracy, and ability to plan time off depend on correct implementation of all rules."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Approve/reject workflow must be intuitive. They need visibility into team leave schedules for resource planning."},{"group":"HR Department","impact":"Owns policy configuration (accrual rate, carry-over cap). Needs audit trails for compliance. Handles disputes arising from forfeiture or rejections."},{"group":"Finance/Payroll","impact":"Leave balances represent financial liabilities. Year-end forfeiture affects balance sheet. Accurate accrual data feeds into payroll processing."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Medical leave documentation requirements must satisfy labor law. Contract end-date enforcement prevents post-employment liability."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The specification is well-structured and internally consistent with no contradictions. Most rules are explicit and testable. Confidence is reduced from high because several operational details are unspecified: partial month accrual, contract end-date inclusivity, carry-over sequencing with pending leave, and the definition of 'working days' (company-specific holidays). These are normal implementation-level ambiguities in an otherwise solid spec.","high_confidence_areas":["No-negative-balance invariant is unambiguous","Accrual rate calculation for complete months","3-day advance notice for non-medical leave","Rejected leave does not consume balance","10-day carry-over cap with forfeiture","Medical leave retroactive exception requires doctor's note within 5 days"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partial month accrual policy not specified — pro-rata vs full-month-only is a meaningful difference","Whether contract end date is inclusive or exclusive for leave eligibility","Sequencing of year-end forfeiture vs pending/in-progress leave consumption","Definition of company working days (holiday calendar) not provided","Whether the 5-day medical leave window uses calendar days or working days (spec says 'days' — assumed calendar)","Whether balance deduction happens at approval time or at leave consumption time"],"assumptions":["'Month worked' means a complete calendar month for accrual purposes","Contract end date is exclusive — the last day of employment is the day before","The 5-day retroactive window for medical leave counts calendar days, not working days","Leave balance is deducted at the time of manager approval, not when leave is actually taken","Working days are Monday–Friday excluding company-recognized public holidays","Year-end carry-over is processed after all December leave is consumed"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs how employees accrue paid leave, request time off, and how unused leave is handled at year-end. This is a classic accrual-based leave system with manager approval workflow and safeguards against balance overdraft.","bounded_context":"Leave Management — owns accrual calculations, leave balances, leave request lifecycle, and year-end carry-over. Interfaces with Employee/Contract context (contract

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this HR leave management domain thoroughly.

Business rules extracted:
1. Accrual: 1.5 days/month worked
2. No negative balance (invariant)
3. 3 working days advance notice required
4. Medical leave exception: retroactive within 5 days with doctor's note
5. Managers approve/reject
6. Rejected leave doesn't consume balance
7. Leave cannot span past contract end date
8. Carry-over cap: max 10 days, rest forfeited

No contradictions. Rules are fairly clear. 

There are a few minor ambiguities around what constitutes a "month worked," how "working days" are defined, and the exact timing of forfeiture, but these are typical clarification questions rather than fundamental issues. I'd rate this domain as well-specified overall, around 72% confidence.
